title: epilobium billardiereanum subsp. hydrophilum
intro: Epilobium billardiereanum subsp. hydrophilum, also known as swamp willowherb, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the willowherb family (Onagraceae). It is a perennial plant that grows in wetlands and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The plant is known to have various health benefits, including reducing urinary tract infections, alleviating symptoms of menopause, and treating conditions such as kidney stones and benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH). Epilobium billardiereanum subsp. hydrophilum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or by extracting its active compounds using a solvent like ethanol.
